title: Marketing Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Reporting, A/B test setup, scheduling, and template creation are largely automatable, but brand strategy and cross-channel judgment still gate most of the role's core work.
rationales.toolMaturity: Marketing automation and AI campaign platforms are mature, widely-shipped products embedded in the standard marketing stack.
rationales.adoption: AI-assisted execution is now a mainstream expectation for marketing teams across most mid-size and large employers.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can own reporting, test setup, and scheduling workflows end-to-end, while positioning, persona synthesis, and channel strategy stay with the manager.

tasksAtRisk: A/B test setup and basic analysis
tasksGrowing: Customer insight synthesis and persona development
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-2021 Marketing Managers, p25-p75 rounded
insight: Execution and reporting now run largely on autopilot. Marketing managers who own positioning, customer insight, and orchestration across an ever-multiplying set of channels are becoming more valuable, not less.
